description.p: PaneFlow is a GPU-accelerated terminal multiplexer for Linux. It splits your terminal into panes and workspaces rendered natively with Vulkan via Zed's GPUI framework — no host terminal emulator required.

ul.li: Horizontal and vertical split panes with drag-to-resize, up to 32 panes
Up to 20 workspaces with rename and quick-switch shortcuts
Session persistence — layouts, working directories, and workspace names restored on relaunch
Hot-reloading themes (Catppuccin Mocha, One Dark, Dracula, Gruvbox Dark, Solarized Dark)
Customizable keybindings via JSON configuration
Unix socket JSON-RPC 2.0 IPC for scripting and AI agent integration
Native Wayland and X11 support

description.p: Linux release hardening patch — closes 10 debt items from v0.2.0.

ul.li: Per-package RPM signing re-enabled — `rpm --checksig` now returns OK on every shipped .rpm
Release → repo-publish auto-chain via workflow_run (no more manual dispatch after each tag)
Post-publish Ubuntu + Fedora end-to-end smoke tests in CI
reprepro arch-idempotency CI regression guard
cargo-deny advisory scanning on every PR (warn-only)
Windows Explorer reveal-in-file-manager argument concatenation fix
GitHub Actions upgraded to Node.js 24 runtime

description.p: Linux packaging migration release — complete Phase 1 + Phase 2.

ul.li: Native .deb, .rpm, .AppImage, and .tar.gz artifacts for x86_64 and aarch64
GPG-signed APT + RPM repositories hosted at pkg.paneflow.dev
Per-install-method auto-updater with 30s network timeout and SHA256 integrity verification
SHA256 sidecars on every release asset
Cross-platform polish: per-OS reveal-in-file-manager dispatch, macOS Core Text font fallback
Sidebar: empty state, clear-all, drag-and-drop reorder
Tabs: double-click the tab bar to open a new terminal
